sections.paragraph:
(“see, i.e. behold, a son!” although the author of the book of Genesis, Gen 29:32, seems so to speak of this name as if it were for רָאוּ (for רָאוּי) בְּעָנְיִי “provided for my affliction,” compare the root No. 2, letter e, [there is nothing in the sacred text to interfere with the simple etymology of this word]), pr.n. Reuben, the eldest son of Jacob, (although deprived of the right of primogeniture, Gen 49:4), and the ancestor of the tribe of the same name; as to their location beyond Jordan, see Num 32:33, seqq.; Jos 13:15.

[Patron. רְאוּבֵנִי Num 26:7, etc.]
